title: senecio breviramulus
intro: Senecio breviramulus, also known as the short-stemmed ragwort, is a plant species within the Senecio genus, characterized by its small stature and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its hepatotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids which are not safe for internal use and should be used with caution. However, Senecio breviramulus has been used in traditional medicine for its purported ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Due to its toxic alkaloids, the plant is not typically prepared for internal consumption, but it is sometimes used in topical herbal preparations and skincare products.
